touches.ai_summary: Initial LinkedIn outreach to Sarah Santos introducing our conversation intelligence platform. Mentioned we help healthcare IT teams gain pipeline visibility. Sarah responded positively and agreed to connect.
next_steps: Schedule qualification call

touches.ai_summary: 17-minute qualification call with Sarah Santos, Head of RevOps. Discussed current challenges with sales visibility and forecasting accuracy. Sarah mentioned they're currently evaluating solutions including Fireflies. She expressed interest in understanding how our platform differs, particularly around RevOps-specific workflows.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform handle integration with our existing Salesforce instance?
questions_asked.answer: Native bi-directional sync, updates in real-time, no manual data entry required

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for tracking deal health and rep activity?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual - reps update Salesforce, but it's inconsistent. Hard to get real visibility into what's actually happening on calls

objections: We're already looking at Fireflies - what makes you different?
next_steps: Sarah to share more details about team size and use case
Schedule follow-up to discuss technical requirements

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call with Sarah. Dove deeper into their RevOps team structure (25 users across sales and CS) and specific pain points around pipeline reviews. Sarah confirmed budget authority sits with her but mentioned IT Director Elijah Roberts will need to review security and integrations.

questions_asked.question: What's driving this evaluation now - is there a specific event or deadline?
questions_asked.answer: Our Q2 planning showed we're missing forecast by 15-20% consistently. Leadership wants better visibility before next quarter

questions_asked.question: Can we get this deployed in the next 30 days?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, typical deployment is 1-2 weeks for a team your size

next_steps: Send overview deck tailored to healthcare IT
Loop in technical team for evaluation

touches.ai_summary: Sent follow-up email to Sarah with customized deck highlighting RevOps use cases and healthcare IT customer examples. Included ROI calculator showing potential impact on forecast accuracy and rep productivity.

questions_asked.question: Do you have any references from similar-sized healthcare IT companies?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, included two case studies in the deck - will set up customer reference calls if helpful

next_steps: Sarah to review deck with team
Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Brief check-in email confirming discovery call timing and requesting Sarah share any specific questions from her team ahead of time. Sarah confirmed attendance and mentioned she'd like to focus on automation capabilities.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for Thursday

touches.ai_summary: 17-minute call to prepare for deeper discovery session. Sarah walked through their current tech stack and mentioned Elijah Roberts (IT Director) will need to be involved for security review. Discussed timeline - they want to make a decision by end of quarter.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in the evaluation beyond you and Elijah?
questions_asked.answer: Probably just us two for now. I own the budget and Elijah handles technical approval

questions_asked.question: What's your pricing structure - is it per seat or platform fee?
questions_asked.answer: Per seat pricing with volume discounts. Will provide detailed proposal after discovery

next_steps: Full discovery call with detailed use case walkthrough
Prepare technical overview for Elijah

touches.ai_summary: 19-minute discovery call diving into specific workflows. Sarah detailed their current manual process for pipeline reviews and how reps struggle to keep Salesforce updated. Discussed automations for call logging, deal summaries, and risk detection. Sarah seemed particularly interested in competitive intelligence features.

questions_asked.question: How does the automation work - does it require reps to do anything differently?
questions_asked.answer: Completely automated. Joins calls, processes transcripts, updates CRM. Reps just take calls normally

questions_asked.question: You mentioned Fireflies earlier - what's attractive about their solution?
questions_asked.answer: Mainly price point. They're cheaper but Sarah acknowledged they don't have the RevOps-specific features we need

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the automations for our specific workflows?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, consulting package includes workflow customization and ongoing optimization

objections: Price will be a consideration - we need to stay within budget
next_steps: Send detailed use case summary
Schedule technical evaluation with Elijah
Prepare pricing proposal

touches.ai_summary: 13-minute follow-up call to address questions Sarah had after reviewing materials. Discussed implementation timeline and change management for the team. Sarah mentioned she wants to move quickly but needs to get Elijah comfortable with the technical side first.

questions_asked.question: What does onboarding look like for the team?
questions_asked.answer: 1-week setup, then 2 weeks of guided onboarding with training sessions. Plus ongoing support

questions_asked.question: Is there an executive sponsor we should involve, or does this decision sit with you?
questions_asked.answer: I have budget authority for this. My VP knows we're evaluating tools but I'll handle the vendor selection

next_steps: Coordinate technical eval with Elijah
Sarah to review internal budget allocation

touches.ai_summary: Email to Sarah confirming technical evaluation scheduled with Elijah for next week. Included technical overview doc covering security, integrations, and deployment architecture. Also attached demo environment access for Sarah's team to test.
next_steps: Technical evaluation with Elijah Roberts
Sarah's team to test demo environment

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sharing additional resources Sarah requested: data privacy compliance docs for healthcare and integration guide for their Salesforce + Outreach stack.

questions_asked.question: Do you have SOC 2 and HIPAA compliance documentation?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, attached compliance package. We're SOC 2 Type II certified and HIPAA compliant

next_steps: Elijah to review technical docs before eval call

touches.ai_summary: 39-minute technical evaluation with Elijah Roberts, IT Director. Covered security architecture, data handling, SSO integration, and API capabilities. Elijah asked detailed questions about data residency and access controls. He seemed satisfied with technical capabilities but noted he's not the economic buyer. Sarah joined last 10 minutes to align on next steps.

questions_asked.question: Where is call data stored and how long is it retained?
questions_asked.answer: US-based AWS infrastructure, configurable retention policies, customer owns all data

questions_asked.question: How does SSO integration work with our Okta setup?
questions_asked.answer: Native SAML integration, typically 1-day setup. Provided implementation guide

questions_asked.question: What API rate limits exist and can we extract our data programmatically?
questions_asked.answer: Enterprise tier has no rate limits. Full API access to all your data with webhooks for real-time events

questions_asked.question: Any technical blockers or concerns from your perspective?
questions_asked.answer: No major concerns. Looks solid technically. I'll need to sign off on the security review paperwork

next_steps: Send security review documentation to Elijah
Sarah to finalize proposal requirements
Move to proposal stage

touches.ai_summary: Email to Sarah with formal proposal for 25 seats, automations plus consulting package. Total ACV $21,500 (20% discount applied to meet her budget constraints). Included ROI analysis showing 6-month payback based on their team size and current forecast accuracy issues.
next_steps: Sarah to review proposal with finance
Schedule call to walk through pricing and terms

touches.ai_summary: 33-minute meeting with Sarah to review proposal in detail. Walked through pricing, contract terms, and implementation timeline. Sarah appreciated the discount but mentioned she needs to validate budget availability. No other executives joined the call despite this being positioned as 'exec meeting.' Discussed payment terms and annual vs. multi-year commitment.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on payment terms - can we do quarterly instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: We can do quarterly for a small increase in total price, or net-60 annual payment

questions_asked.question: Should we have your VP or CFO involved in this conversation given the investment size?
questions_asked.answer: Not necessary - I have authority to approve this level of spend. Just need to confirm final budget allocation

questions_asked.question: What happens if we want to add more seats mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: Simple amendment process, pro-rated pricing for remainder of term

objections: Need to make sure this fits in Q2 budget - may need to adjust scope or timing
next_steps: Sarah to confirm budget approval by end of week
Prepare MSA and order form

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email after proposal review call. Sent MSA and order form for legal review. Reiterated value proposition and ROI to help with internal business case.
next_steps: Sarah to route contracts to legal and finance

touches.ai_summary: Check-in email on contract review status. Sarah responded that legal is reviewing but she's working on final budget approval. Mentioned timeline may slip slightly into next quarter.

questions_asked.question: If we push to Q3, would pricing stay the same?
questions_asked.answer: Discount was tied to Q2 close, but can extend for 2 weeks into Q3

next_steps: Sarah to follow up with finance on budget
Legal review in progress

touches.ai_summary: Email sharing additional customer success story from healthcare IT company that achieved 25% improvement in forecast accuracy. Reinforcing business case as Sarah works through internal approvals.
next_steps: Sarah to finalize budget discussions

touches.ai_summary: Email from Sarah indicating legal has some redlines on the MSA, primarily around data processing and termination clauses. She also mentioned finance is questioning the discount level and asked if we're negotiable on price. Moving to negotiation stage.

questions_asked.question: Can we adjust pricing or do we need to cut scope to lower total cost?
questions_asked.answer: Let's discuss on a call - we have some flexibility but the 20% discount is already significant

next_steps: Schedule negotiation call
Review legal redlines

touches.ai_summary: 43-minute meeting with Sarah to address legal redlines and pricing concerns. Reviewed contract changes - agreed to most data processing amendments. On pricing, Sarah pushed for additional 10% discount citing competitive pressure from Fireflies. Discussed value differentiation vs. Fireflies again. Sarah mentioned she's advocating internally but facing questions about ROI timeline. Still no executive involvement in the discussions.

questions_asked.question: Fireflies is coming in 40% cheaper - can you match or get closer to their pricing?
questions_asked.answer: Fireflies is a recording tool, we're a full RevOps automation platform. That said, we can explore a smaller reduction if it helps close the gap

questions_asked.question: What would make this a clear yes for you and your team?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, if we can get the number down another 5-7% I think I can get this approved. And we need flexible payment terms

questions_asked.question: Can we do a 6-month pilot instead of annual commitment?
questions_asked.answer: Our platform requires annual minimum for the consulting package, but we can build in an exit clause at 6 months

objections: Finance is questioning whether we need all the premium features
next_steps: Revise proposal with adjusted pricing and payment terms
Sarah to socialize revised terms internally

touches.ai_summary: 32-minute negotiation call focused on contract terms and final pricing. Discussed reducing total to $19,800 (additional 8% off, 27% total discount) with quarterly payments. Sarah seemed more comfortable at this price point. Worked through remaining legal items - agreed to mutual termination for convenience clause and adjusted data retention terms.

questions_asked.question: At this price, can we still get the full consulting package or is that reduced?
questions_asked.answer: Full consulting package included - we want you to be successful. This is our best and final offer

questions_asked.question: Is there anything else blocking this from your side once we align on these terms?
questions_asked.answer: Just final finance signoff. I'm confident at this number

next_steps: Send revised proposal and contracts
Sarah to get finance approval
Target signature by end of week

touches.ai_summary: 28-minute call to finalize contract details. Reviewed updated MSA with negotiated terms. Sarah confirmed she's comfortable with the deal structure and pricing. Discussed implementation kickoff timeline assuming contracts signed this week. However, Sarah mentioned she's still working to get final finance approval and asked about extending the deadline.

questions_asked.question: If finance needs one more week to process, can we keep this pricing locked?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can hold pricing through end of month

questions_asked.question: Is there anyone else we should be talking to on your team to help move this forward?
questions_asked.answer: No, I'm managing it internally. Just need a bit more time for the approval workflow

next_steps: Sarah to submit for final finance approval
Contracts ready for signature
Implementation team on standby

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email confirming contracts are ready for signature and reiterating pricing hold through end of month. Included implementation kickoff plan to maintain momentum. Sarah acknowledged receipt and said she's working through final approvals.
next_steps: Sarah to complete internal approval process
Await signature
